name: Basic Pizza Dough
recipeInstructions:
1. Measure 1/2 cup warm water (110°F) into a container and stir in the brown sugar. Make sure the water is warm, not hot; too hot will kill the yeast.
2. Dissolve the 2 packages of dried yeast in the water and set aside for 5 minutes. The mixture should become frothy.
3. Sift 4 cups of flour and salt into a large mixing bowl.
4. Make a depression in the middle of the flour and pour in 3/4 cup olive oil and 1 cup warm water. When the yeast is ready, add it as well.
5. Dust your kneading surface with flour, then mix the ingredients in the bowl with your hands. Place dough ball on the floured surface and knead for 8 to 10 minutes. Add more flour if the dough is too sticky or wet. The dough should become elastic.
6. Rub the insides of a clean bowl with olive oil and place the dough inside, coating it thoroughly. Cover with a clean cloth and let rise in a warm, draft-free place until doubled in size, about 1 1/2 to 2 hours.
7. When the dough has risen, divide into two halves. Roll each out on a floured surface. A round shape may be cut out using a table knife and a 12-inch bowl or plate as a template.
8. This recipe yields enough for 2 12-inch thin-crust pizzas, or 1 12-inch thick-crust pizza.
9. Before filling and baking, form a rim around the outer edge of the pizza dough to hold ingredients better. Brush the inside area of the dough with olive oil and let it rise a little more before filling.
10. Spoon sauce into the center, cover with 1 lb mozzarella cheese, add meat toppings, then cover with a mixture of mozzarella and parmesan cheese.
11. Brush the rim of the pizza crust with olive oil. Sprinkle cornmeal on the pizza pan or oven tiles to prevent sticking.
12. For best results, cook on a pizza stone or oven tile at 500°F. The unglazed oven tile helps create a crispier crust and even moisture distribution. It may be removed with the pizza and set on a rack on the table to keep it warm longer.
